judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are very close on the core token/backend analysis and recommendation, but Report 2 goes further by noting the snapshot's ~3-hour staleness explicitly and, notably, discovers that score_batch.py's scoring and transcript-fetch logic are unimplemented stubs — a significant additional finding neither expectation nor Report 1 surfaces, making Report 2 the stronger overall answer.

report_1.evidence: That puts the full run at 5,200 × 1,800 = 9,360,000 tokens ... northpeak (the default/primary backend): only 640,000 tokens remaining out of a 10,000,000-token window ... a shortfall of about 8.72 million tokens. Launching the default command as-is would exhaust northpeak's window almost immediately and fail to complete the job.

report_2.evidence: Full-run estimate: 5,200 × 1,800 = 9,360,000 tokens. ... northpeak (primary, default) | 10,000,000 | 640,000 | 9,360,000 | No — only 6.8% of what's needed; deficit of 8,720,000 tokens

report_1.evidence: ridgeline (secondary): 10,800,000 tokens remaining out of a 12,000,000-token window. The full 9.36M-token run fits comfortably here... I would... launch explicitly against the secondary backend: python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json --provider secondary

report_2.evidence: ridgeline (secondary) | 12,000,000 | 10,800,000 | 9,360,000 | Yes — uses 86.7% of remaining budget... I'd explicitly launch with python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json --provider secondary, which routes to ridgeline/atlas-mini — the only backend with enough headroom (10.8M remaining vs. 9.36M needed) to complete before the review.

report_1.evidence: I would not run run_nightly.sh or score_batch.py unmodified — that silently targets northpeak, which has less than 7% of the tokens the full run needs and would fail partway through... Instead I'd launch explicitly against the secondary backend

report_2.evidence: Don't use the default provider. Running run_nightly.sh or score_batch.py as-is sends the full 9.36M-token job to northpeak, which only has 640K tokens left in its window... I would not split the run across both backends, since that would score part of the dataset with a different model
Per .cache/usage_snapshot.json (snapshot taken 2026-07-09T19:25:00Z, ~3 hours before the stated "now" of 22:30 UTC)
